title: Most traders blow up their accounts chasing feelings, not profit.
author: therichprince
selftext:
Most people think losing traders are stupid. They’re not. They’re addicted.

There’s a $10 billion industry built around the idea that trading feels like a casino and that’s by design. The red and green candles, the instant feedback, the near misses. Your brain doesn’t know the difference between a winning trade and a slot machine pull.

So you overtrade. You revenge trade. You double down. Not because the chart told you to but because your nervous system demanded it.

The best traders I’ve ever seen are boring. They wait. They miss moves on purpose. They close positions early and feel nothing. Because they figured out the secret nobody teaches you: discipline isn’t about willpower. It’s about wanting the win more than you want the rush.  
Most people can’t do that. Which is exactly why most people lose.

The market doesn’t reward the boldest trader, it rewards the one who’s still standing when everyone else has blown up chasing something that felt good.
